title: Allergic Bronchopulmonary Aspergillosis
chief_complaint: I have asthma but it's not responding to my usual inhalers. I'm coughing up brownish mucus plugs that look like rubber. I've had recurrent pneumonias.

physical_exam.Lungs: Diffuse wheezing. Prolonged expiration. Coarse crackles in right upper lobe.

hidden_data.cxr: Right upper lobe infiltrates with central bronchiectasis (tram-track sign).
hidden_data.ct_chest: Central (proximal) bronchiectasis in upper lobes. Mucus plugging with high-attenuation mucus (pathognomonic for ABPA). Tree-in-bud opacities.
hidden_data.aspergillus_ige: Aspergillus fumigatus-specific IgE: Positive. Aspergillus IgG: Elevated.
hidden_data.pft: Moderate obstructive pattern. FEV1 55%. Bronchodilator response positive.

key_learning_points: ABPA = hypersensitivity reaction to Aspergillus fumigatus in patients with asthma or CF. NOT an infection
Diagnostic criteria: Asthma + Central bronchiectasis + Elevated total IgE (>1,000) + Positive Aspergillus-specific IgE/IgG + Peripheral eosinophilia
High-attenuation mucus on CT is pathognomonic (calcified mucus plugs). Central bronchiectasis is hallmark (proximal airway dilation)
Treatment: Prednisone 0.5mg/kg/day taper over 3-6 months (reduces inflammation). Itraconazole 200mg BID ×4 months (reduces fungal burden). Monitor IgE levels to track response
Do NOT use inhaled antifungals. Re-exacerbation common. Monitor IgE every 3-6 months. IgE rising = impending exacerbation
